topic.what: Green-red watermarking framework (GREW) for sequential recommender systems that partitions item space into green/red sets using secret key
topic.how: Integrates Semantic-Consistent Hashing, Decision-Aligned Masking, and Confidence-Adaptive Scaling to embed watermark signals aligned with model's intrinsic ranking logic
topic.why_matters: Enables robust IP protection for recommender models against extraction attacks without data injection or performance degradation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ownership claim success rate on watermarked models
characteristics.vs_baseline: AOW achieves 100% on teacher model but 0-26% on extracted model
characteristics.evidence: Table 1 - V@1 metrics across BERT, SAS, NARM on ML-1M, Steam, Amazon Beauty
characteristics.context: 100% verification across all three base models and three datasets

characteristics.what_it_measures: Watermark retention after DFME model extraction attack
characteristics.vs_baseline: AOW drops to 0% on extracted models; GREW maintains verification even under heterogeneous extraction
characteristics.evidence: Table 1, Table 3 - V@1 on extracted models
characteristics.context: GREW survives heterogeneous extraction (NARM->BERT) while AOW fails homogeneous extraction (BERT->BERT)

characteristics.vs_baseline: Within 1-3% of unwatermarked base models; AOW shows 4%+ degradation on NARM
characteristics.context: NARM with GREW: 26.29% vs Base: 27.56% vs AOW: 23.15% on ML-1M

characteristics.what_it_measures: Statistical significance of green item deviation
characteristics.vs_baseline: Threshold Z>4 corresponds to P<10^-5; GREW far exceeds threshold
characteristics.evidence: Table 2 - Z@20 scores and P-values across models
characteristics.context: SASRec achieves highest Z=182.02 with P<10^-6; all models exceed Z>86

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of training data requiring synthetic trigger injection
characteristics.context: GREW eliminates data injection requirement entirely

characteristics.what_it_measures: Adversary detection rate via anomaly detection
characteristics.vs_baseline: AOW achieves 100% detectability by simple popularity/transition detector
characteristics.context: AOW's 10% injection of OOD patterns is fully exposed; GREW's semantic approach undetectable
